dsl_rule.translation_notes: Breakaway gap modeled as close crossing previous high/low with above-average volume. Entry conditions detect gap occurrence; exit conditions use reversal of the gap direction. Retracement rates (1%-6%) are incorporated via stop_loss_pct at conservative 1.0% to capture low retracement expectation. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both upside and downside gaps. Target regimes set to TRENDING to reflect trend continuation expectation.

backtest_rule.reason: Breakaway gap modeled as close crossing previous high/low with above-average volume. Entry conditions detect gap occurrence; exit conditions use reversal of the gap direction. Retracement rates (1%-6%) are incorporated via stop_loss_pct at conservative 1.0% to capture low retracement expectation. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both upside and downside gaps. Target regimes set to TRENDING to reflect trend continuation expectation.

source_rule_text: A breakaway gap, which occurs when prices move out of a sideways range, marks the beginning of a new trend and should be traded with expectation of trend continuation based on low retracement rates (1% and 6%).
